Title: Ashe
Age: Current24 years old Timeline Born973 AN Edit

children.title: Early Life
children.textContent: Ashe was born to Grena, matriarch of the warlike Avarosan tribe. From a young age, she was placed under immense pressure to one day succeed her mother as chieftain, leaving her feeling burdened and isolated. Her main respite was another Iceborn girl, Sejuani, the granddaughter of Hejian, chieftain of the Winter's Claw, the sister tribe of the Avarosans. Sejuani stayed with them during the summer months and their friendship defined their childhoods. However, their friendship was cut short when they entered their teenage years, after Hejian severed ties with the Avarosans when Grena questioned her treatment of Sejuani.

children.title: Reuniting with Sejuani
children.textContent:
When Ashe returned to her village, she found it razed to the ground and all the Avarosans killed. With her tribe gone, Ashe ventured into Ursine territory in search of food. She quickly found a dead beast, but it had been bait for a grellfeen. The creature nearly devoured Ashe before she was saved by a girl she to believed to be an Ursine. Though the girl landed several strikes against the grellfeen, she couldn't bring it down. Ashe froze it in place with her bow, allowing the girl to finish it off.

With the beast dead, Ashe apologized for intruding on Ursine lands, but the girl revealed herself to be Ashe's childhood friend, Sejuani. Though Sejuani was happy to be reunited with her, she questioned why Ashe was here, to which she revealed the fate of her tribe. Together, they butchered the grellfeen, but were soon beset by real Ursine. They carried the meat onto Sejuani's boat before the Ursine could catch them and escaped into the sea.

As they journeyed back to Winter's Claw, Ashe explained how her tribe had been betrayed by a frost priest, something Sejuani believed was also happening to her tribe. In the weeks that followed, Ashe and Sejuani reconnected during their voyage, with Sejuani promising one night that they would be sisters.

When they reached the waters of the Winter's Claw, Sejuani warned Ashe that they would need to win over the tribe before the frost priests discovered her true identity. They planned to distribute the food they had gathered, but Sejuani's mother, Kalkia, was already waiting for them at the docks. The frost priests cautioned the warmother against trusting outsiders, but Kalkia was willing to grant Ashe shelter if she explained where the food had come from. Ashe lied, claiming that they had hunted the grellfeen in open waters after it attacked her ship, and that Sejuani had saved her. The frost priests saw through the lie, but Sejuani lied to confirm her story. Kalkia demanded to speak with her daughter in private, while Ashe began distributing the meat to the tribe.

Later that night, the frost priests confronted Ashe during a feast. They accused her of heresy for pursuing the Throne of Avarosa, but Sejuani declared Ashe her battlesister and would prove her worth in the next hunt. The frost priests objected, but Kalkia allowed her daughter's declaration and would decide what to do with Ashe in the morning.

Believing they could not wait, Sejuani brought Ashe to her hunting party. She feared that the frost priests would strike before morning or her mother would turn Ashe over and decided that they should head out now. Ashe considered returning to sea, but Sejuani insisted she stay, wanting to protect her just as Grena had protected her when she was younger. Sejuani drew a knife and cut her palm, swearing a blood oath to Ashe. She promised to protect Ashe and named her her battlesister. Ashe then took the knife and did the same, declaring Sejuani her battlesister in return.

children.title: The New Avarosans
children.textContent:
Sejuani led the hunting party to the Ebrataal, a neighboring tribe that shared the Winter's Claw hunting grounds. While Ashe believed they intended only to steal supplies, Sejuani sought to end the food shortage permanently and ordered her warriors to burn the village. Appalled as the Winter's Claw began killing the tribespeople, Ashe intervened, firing an ice arrow at one of Sejuani's warriors. She pleaded with Sejuani to stop, but Sejuani revealed that the frost priests had forced her mother into a treaty with the Ebrataal. In order to not be labeled oathbreakers, they could not leave any witnesses alive. Sejuani ordered Ashe to continue, but Ashe refused and turned her bow on Sejuani instead.

Ashe pleaded for the villagers' lives, insisting that they could survive at sea, just as she had. Sejuani argued that Ashe had survived only because destiny had led her to her, but Ashe rejected the idea of destiny and loosed her arrows at Sejuani. As they fought, Sejuani declared that it was Ashe's fate to be her battlesister and that the Ebrataal were nothing, sacrifices that had to be made for her tribe to survive. Sejuani told her that Grena was her hero and that she was doing exactly what she would have done, but Ashe declared that her mother was failure, because all lives had meaning.

In turn, Sejuani accused Ashe of being the one failed by Grena, telling that she envied how much Grena had loved her when her own mother did not, but that such love had made Ashe soft. She argued that Ashe could not lead if she was unwilling to lie, kill, and make sacrifices, but Ashe reminded Sejuani of her own words: "It is the strong who must try the impossible." Ashe stressed that Sejuani had brought her on the hunt to earn the title of battlesister, but that she could not do so through oathbreaking and the slaughter of an entire village. She promised to help Sejuani become warmother, but as her sister, she begged her to let her honor her words and try to lead the Ebrataal to safety.

Sejuani issued a final ultimatum: Ashe could either stand with her as her sister, or watch the Ebrataal fail. Ashe answered that she had to try. Reluctantly, Sejuani allowed Ashe to take the surviving Ebrataal, but warned her that if they ever crossed paths again, she would be considered an enemy. As Sejuani and her warriors departed, Ashe called after her, declaring that she would always be her sister and never her enemy, but Sejuani replied that Ashe would have no choice.

In the aftermath, the surviving Ebrataal pledged themselves to Ashe. However, Ashe refused to take them as slaves, and instead declared them Avarosans. Though they had no supplies, Ashe and the new Avarosans set sail south. Along the way, more hearthbound would join the Avarosans. When asked how she wished to be addressed, Ashe declared that she was their warmother.

As the Avarosans took in more people, rumors began circulating that Ashe not only wielded Avarosa's bow, but she was also the reincarnation of the legend herself. Ashe leveraged the myth in order to make alliances with the southern tribes to feed her burgeoning tribe and wanted to unite them into a nation that could challenge even the neighboring kingdoms.

children.title: Saving Tryndamere
children.textContent:
With more tribes allying themselves with the Avarosans, Ashe soon found herself in the center of a political feud. As a warmother, she was expected to take a husband from one from one of the major tribes, however, regardless of who she chose it would still anger the others. Her answer finally came in the form of a warrior from an impoverished tribe named Tryndamere. Seeking the Avarosan's protection for his people, Tryndamere challenged many contenders to duels in order to prove his tribe's worth. During a duel at Rakelstake, Tryndamere became consumed by his bloodlust and was only stopped by the timely intervention of Braum, who later introduced him to Ashe.

Impressed by his ferocity, Ashe decided to take Tryndamere as her first and only bloodsworn. In exchange for adopting his tribe, Tryndamere would serve as her champion, warding off any who sought her hand in marriage or challenged her rule. Though they initially wed out of political gain, true affection began to bloom between the two. Shortly after they wed, Ashe saved Tryndamere's life as he battled against raiders and death itself.

As more tribes joined Ashe's coalition, it earned the ire of Sejuani, now warmother of the Winter's Claw. Believing that Ashe tarnished Grena's legacy, Sejuani sought to destroy the Avarosans and prove that only she was worthy of ruling the Freljord.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Ashe is a slender, pale-skinned woman with blue eyes and long white hair. She wears light, elegant robes with a distinct palette of black, dark blue, and gold. She also wields a Avarosa's True Ice Bow.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
Ashe is smart, stoic, serene and has a calm disposition. She is idealistic as well, believing that by retaking their ancient, tribal lands once more will unite the Freljord.

However, Ashe is implied to have a heated temper and vengeful streak, especially to those who have hurt her allies in the past. Her rage is so intense that she relies on the stinging cold of True Ice to keep her calm.

children.title: Sejuani
children.textContent: Ashe and Sejuani were childhood friends but were separated when Ashe's mother offended Sejuani's grandmother about how she was raising Sejuani. They were eventually reunited as teenagers after Ashe's tribe was decimated by Lissandra's frost priests, but the two had a falling out during a raid against a neighbor of the Winter's Claw. These neighbors, whom Ashe did not want to kill, would later become the first members of the new Avarosans. The two once saw each other as sisters.

children.title: Trundle
children.textContent: Legends of Runeterra cards suggest Ashe's Avarosan and Trundle's trolls are in direct conflict which has resulted in a number of loss for the tribe. For that reason, Ashe wants the race to be exterminated.

children.title: Anivia
children.textContent: Anivia is the demigod that Ashe prays to the most and the one she has the deepest connection to. This is because she speaks the most to Ashe's hopes and beliefs. Rumors say a recently hatched Anivia has met Ashe, and perhaps Anivia sees one who may be able to finally reunite the Freljord.

children.title: Nunu and  Willump
children.textContent: It is unknown if Nunu and Willump support Ashe, but their deeds around the Freljord lead towards the same goal.

children.title: Tryndamere
children.textContent: Ashe and Tryndamere are Bloodsworn, a political marriage, but the two eventually developed genuine affection for each other. Ashe originally married Tryndamere in order to avoid having to marry multiple suitors from neighboring tribes. All of this is, in turn, an inside reference at Riot. The name of Riot's co-founder Marc 'Tryndamere' Merrill's wife is Ashley.

children.title: Lissandra
children.textContent: Unbeknownst to all, Lissandra is the Ice Witch of Freljordian legend, who is actually waiting for the right moment to strike Ashe's and Sejuani's tribes and wipe out any potential incarnations of Avarosa and Serylda in order to take over the Freljord. As revealed by Lissandra, she herself is one of the Three Sisters who led the Iceborn uprising against the Watchers. Whether or not she knows Ashe and Sejuani are potentially direct descendants of her sisters (Avarosa and Serylda, respectively) making her their great-aunt-many-times-removed, is unknown.

mainContent.textContent:
